articles.title: R to @AnthropicAI: Globally, 67% of people view AI positively, but optimism runs higher in South America, Africa, and Asia than in Europe or the United States.
articles.summary: Globally, 67% of people view AI positively, but optimism runs higher in South America, Africa, and Asia than in Europe or the United States.

articles.title:
R to @AnthropicAI: What people wanted and feared from AI appeared tightly bound. Those who benefited the most from AI in a given area were also the most likely to fear what it could cost them.

But while mentions of benefits tend to be grounded in experience, fears were more often anticipatory.
articles.summary:

articles.title:
R to @AnthropicAI: Hopes clustered around a few basic desires, but concerns about AI were more varied. Most common were AI unreliability, jobs and the economy, and maintaining human autonomy and agency. 

Notably, economic concern was the strongest predictor of overall AI sentiment.
articles.summary: Hopes clustered around a few basic desires, but concerns about AI were more varied. Most common were AI unreliability, jobs and the economy, and maintaining human autonomy and agency.

articles.title:
RT by @Fabien_Mikol: This is a pig who spent years confined in a gestation crate. She was left behind when factory farms flooded in Iowa and then rescued by some volunteers.

The volunteers took her home and dug her a mud pit. She ignored it. They assumed years of confinement had extinguished her natural instincts.

Then they noticed her wandering into the woods on their property. They followed her — and found her rooting in a pile of dirt, digging her own mud pit.

The pork industry claims pigs adapt to confinement. They don't. Inside every gestation crate is an animal who still yearns to root, wallow, and just be a pig.

This is the tragedy of factory farming. We tried to reduce feeling animals to machines. We failed.

articles.summary: GPT-5.4 mini and nano are smaller, faster versions of GPT-5.4 optimized for coding, tool use, multimodal reasoning, and high-volume API and sub-agent workloads.

articles.title: Equipping workers with insights about compensation
articles.summary: New research shows Americans send nearly 3 million daily messages to ChatGPT asking about compensation and earnings, helping close the wage information gap.

articles.title: Rakuten fixes issues twice as fast with Codex
articles.summary: Rakuten uses Codex, the coding agent from OpenAI, to ship software faster and safer, reducing MTTR 50%, automating CI/CD reviews, and delivering full-stack builds in weeks.

articles.title: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection
articles.summary: How ChatGPT defends against prompt injection and social engineering by constraining risky actions and protecting sensitive data in agent workflows.

articles.title: Wayfair boosts catalog accuracy and support speed with OpenAI
articles.summary: Wayfair uses OpenAI models to improve ecommerce support and product catalog accuracy, automating ticket triage and enhancing millions of product attributes at scale.

articles.title: From model to agent: Equipping the Responses API with a computer environment
articles.summary: How OpenAI built an agent runtime using the Responses API, shell tool, and hosted containers to run secure, scalable agents with files, tools, and state.

articles.title: R to @AnthropicAI: The Institute will be led by @jackclarkSF, in a new role as Anthropic’s Head of Public Benefit. It'll bring together an interdisciplinary staff of machine learning engineers, economists, and social scientists, making full use of the inside information of a frontier AI lab.

It will lead new research into the challenges posed by more powerful AI, and partner with others to address them.

articles.title:
We partnered with Mozilla to test Claude's ability to find security vulnerabilities in Firefox.

Opus 4.6 found 22 vulnerabilities in just two weeks. Of these, 14 were high-severity, representing a fifth of all high-severity bugs Mozilla remediated in 2025.

En conseil de classe, on entend désormais régulièrement : “Les autres utilisent l’IA… pas mon enfant. C'est injuste !”
Une étude récente montre pourtant que 64 % des ados utilisent des chatbots quand 51% des parents pensent que leur enfant les utilise.

articles.title: Why Codex Security Doesn’t Include a SAST Report
articles.summary: A deep dive into why Codex Security doesn’t rely on traditional SAST, instead using AI-driven constraint reasoning and validation to find real vulnerabilities with fewer false positives.
